California
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornmission
Penny Loper
Sierra sands Unified School District
113 Felspar
Ridgecrest, cA 93555
Re:
Dear Ms. Loper:
February 28, 199O
Request for
our File No. Informal Assistance
r-90-043
You have requested advice concerning the conflicq of interest
code provisions of the Po1itical Reform Act (t'Act").r/ 
Your
request is one for general assistance. Accordingly, we are
treating it as a request for informal .assistance pursuant to
Regulation 1,8329 (c) (copy enclosed) .2/
CONCLUSTONS
Coumission Reg:ulation 18700 sets out the definition of what
it neans to make or participate in naking decisions. That
regulation also provides the definition of a consultant.
The enclosed advice letters may also provide sone gNidance to
you.
l/ 
Governnent code Sections 81ooo-91015. AtI statutory
references are to the GovernDent code unless otherwise indicated.
Coromission regnrlations aPpear at 2 california Code of Regulations
Section 18ooo, et seq. A11 references to regrulations are to Title
z, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2/ rnformat assistance does not provide the requestor with the
inrnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
lcovernrnent code Section 83114; 2 CaI. code of Regs. section
r-832e (c) (3) . )

be included in a conflict
Are there guidelines
consultants are requirEd
OUESTIONS
available to help deternine who should
of interest code?
available to help deternine which
to file conflict of interest statements?
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box EO7 . sacramento cA 95EO4-O8O7 . (916)322-5660


Page Two
February 28′  ■990
FACTS
You are in the process of arnending the conflict of interest
code ("coderr) for the Sierra sands Unified school District. 
There
are several positions you believe may need to be added to the code
but are seeking assistance in deternining what criteria you could
follon to reach that determination. You also need some assistance
in detenrining what types of consultants should be required to
file the statements of econornic interests.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires every state and loca1 agency to adopt a
conflict of interest code enumerating its designated employees
(Sections 87300 and a73o2. ) section 82019 defines a designated
enployee as any officer, employee, member or consultant of any
agency whose position irith the agency is designated in a conflict
of interest code because the position entails the naking or
participation in the uraking of decisions which may foreseeably
affect any financial interest.
A public official 
rrnakes a governmental decisionrr when
he or she, acting r.rithin the authority of his or her office:
(1) votes on a natter;
(2) Appoints a personi
(3) obligates or conmj.ts his or her agency to any
course of action;
(4) Enters into any contractual agreenent on behalf of
his or her agency;
(5) Determines not to act, within the neaning of
sub-paragraphs (1) , (2') , (3) and (4), unless such
deternination is nade because of his or her
financial interest.
Regnrlation 187 00 (b) .
A public official 
rrparticipates in the naking of .a
governm-ental decisionrr lrhen, acting rrithin the authority of
his or her position, he or she:
(1) Negotiates, lrithout significant substantive review,
with a governmental entity or private person
regarding the decision; or


Page Three
February 28, l99O
(2) Advises or makes recommendations to the decision-
maker, either directly or without significant
intervening substantive review, by:
(A) Conducting research or roaking any
investigation which requires the exercise
of judgnnent on the part of the official
and the purpose of which is to
influence the decision; or
(B) Preparing or presentinq any report,
analysis or opinion, orally or in
writing, which reguires the
exercise of judgrment on the part of the
official 
and the purpose of which is to
influence the decision.
Regulation 18700 (c)
you have asked if certain departnent supervisors should be
covered by the code. Since you have not provided job descriptions
or duty stateDents for these positions, we can offer only linited
assistance to you in naking that deternination.
when trying to decide who nakes or participates in naking
decisions, you need to review duty stateuents or job descriptions
for each position and ask yourself: what does this person do?
Does this person make or participate in the naking of decisions?
Does this person make recoDmendations to another person or body?
Does this person vote on any matter? can he or she coDmlt our
agency to any course of action or nake purchasing decisions or
reconnendations? If your ansrrer to any of these is in the
affinnative, then that position should be designated in the code.
I realize this rnay not have provided you with the assistance
you were hoping to obtain. If you rrish to subEit job descriptions
or duty statements for those positions being questioned, rre rrould
be happy to review them and suggest appropriate disclosure.
You will recall that the definition of a "designated
employeert includes rrconsultants.rl
A rrconsultant, r within the meaning of the Act, includes |tany
natural person lrho provides, under contract, infornation, advi.ce,
reconnendation or counsel to a state or local government
agency....rr (Regulation 18700(a) (2).) This tern does not
include, horrever, a person rrho:
(A) Conducts research and arrives at conclusions
with respect to his or her rendition of infornation,
advice, recommendation or counsel independent of the
control and direction of the agency or of any agency
officials, 
other than normal contract monitoring; and


Page Four
February 28′  ■990
(B) Possesses no
agency decision beyond
advice, recommendation
authority with
the rendition
or counsel.
respect to any
of information,
Regulation 187o0 (a) (2) (A) - (B) .
When a consultant is retained to deliver a specific finished
product or just provide information upon r+hich the governing body
nay act, he or she would norDally not be a public official. 
If
the consultant provides advice or counsel on a continuing basis,
or reconmends a specific course of action, that consultant would
be deemed to be a public official 
under the Act.
For exanple, in the Workman Advice Letter No. A-87-078 (copy
enclosed), the Connission advised that a computer evaluation firn
retained to provide advice on a conputer implenentation p1an,
conduct research to determine the current state of the art i,n
computers, determine who are potential vendors for hardware or
software, assist in preparing the specifications for bids,
evaluate bid responses, advise the county on the appropriate bid
to select, and to be available for consultation and training, are
rrconsultantstr under the Act. rn the clifford Advice Letter No. A-
83-103 (copy enclosed), the Comrnission advised that an engineer
retained to construct a filtration 
systeur according to
specifications of a contract where there were no deviations fron
the contract, was being called upon to deliver a finished product,
and was not a rrconsultantrr within the meaning of the Act.
. Enclosed are several other advice letters which further
discuss the reporting by consultants. Hopefully, these letters
will provide you with additional guidance in uaking your
determination.
If you have any further questions, pfease feel free to
contact ne at (916) 322-59OL.
S incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
Political Reform consultant
Legal Division
KED/」ET/jt
